prompt:
Add a custom format to reject dubbed releases in both Sonarr and Radarr, without filtering out original-language foreign films.

## Context
The Andor S2 problem was German-dubbed VVC releases (filename: 'German.DL.1080p.HDR.WEBRip.h266-266ers'). We do NOT want to set the quality profile language to English because that would filter out original-language foreign films (French cinema, Japanese anime, etc.). The user speaks French and prefers subtitled foreign movies.

## Requirements
Add to the media-stack-sync script (modules/media-server/recyclarr.nix) a custom format that penalizes dubbed releases. The custom format should:

1. Be named 'Reject Dubbed Releases' (or similar)
2. Match release title patterns indicating dubs: \b(DUBBED|DUB)\b and also the German dual-language pattern \bDL\b (which means 'Dual Language' in German scene naming)
3. Also match common foreign-language dub tags like \b(FRENCH|GERMAN|SPANISH|ITALIAN|RUSSIAN|PORTUGUESE|HINDI|CHINESE|KOREAN|JAPANESE|ARABIC|TURKISH|POLISH|DUTCH|SWEDISH|NORWEGIAN|DANISH|FINNISH|CZECH|HUNGARIAN|ROMANIAN|THAI|VIETNAMESE|INDONESIAN|MALAY|TAGALOG)\.(?:DL|DUBBED|DUB)\b — i.e. language name followed by DL/DUBBED/DUB
4. Use a score of -10000 on all quality profiles to effectively reject them
5. Be idempotent — check if the custom format already exists before creating

Important: Do NOT use \bDL\b alone as it could match other things. The pattern should specifically target dub indicators.

Actually, the safest patterns are:
- \b(DUBBED|DUB)\b — catches any explicitly dubbed release
- \b\w+\.DL\b — catches the scene convention of 'Language.DL' (Dual Language)

Assign score -10000 to all quality profiles in both Sonarr and Radarr.

Follow the same pattern as the existing ensure_vvc_custom_format function. Add this as a new section in the sync script.

Also check if TRaSH Guides has any relevant custom format IDs for this — if so, prefer adding them to recyclarr.yml instead of manual API calls.

Make sure nixfmt passes.
